title: Craft Table Top for the Modular Collection
summary: A big MDF work surface with two hidden drawers underneath, sized to sit on a pair of modular bookcases — a craft or hobby station that comes apart.
description:
This is the top half of a modular craft table: a large MDF or A1 plywood surface with a two-drawer housing built underneath, designed to rest on two bookcase bases so the whole thing knocks down for moving. It's the kind of piece that gives you a huge flat work zone plus supply storage without a permanent, heavy build.

The make-or-break skill here is square. The drawer housing (the 1x4 framework that carries the slides) must be built perfectly square and fastened to the top perfectly square, because the drawers ride on slides and slides are unforgiving — an out-of-square housing means drawers that bind and jam forever. The source is blunt about it, and it's right: predrill and countersink, take your time, check with a square at every joint.

The screws that fasten the housing to the top are driven from above, countersunk 2" deep so the heads bury into the 1x4s and nothing shows on the work surface. The drawer faces are measured to the actual opening (about 29-1/2", minus 1/4") rather than a nominal number. And notably, the top is NOT glued to the bookcases — screwed only — so you can lift it off and carry the pieces separately. Keep it that way.
timeLabel: 5–7 hrs for the top and drawers

tools.note: Optional — the housing is predrilled and countersunk screws here.
The drawer housing must be dead square or the drawers bind.

cutList.note: Measure the actual opening and subtract 1/4".

steps.title: Build the drawer housing — perfectly square
steps.body: Mark out and cut the housing sides and center. Fasten the center to the sides with 2" screws and glue, predrilling and countersinking. This has to be built dead perfect — the drawers will not slide right if it's off. Check every joint with a square.

steps.title: Fasten the housing to the top
steps.body: Position the housing on the underside of the top per your layout and fasten with 2" screws driven from above, countersunk 2" deep so the heads bury into the 1x4s and nothing shows on the work surface. Keep it square to the top's edges.

steps.title: Build the drawer boxes
steps.body: Fasten each drawer back and sides to the drawer bottom with 2" screws and glue, checking each box for square — a racked box binds on its slides. Cut the bottoms square too.

steps.title: Mount slides and fit the faces
steps.body: Install the drawer slides and slide the boxes in. Measure the opening outside-to-outside (about 29-1/2"), subtract 1/4", and cut each face from a 1x4 to that. Line the faces up on the drawers and attach, then add pulls if you like.

steps.title: Set the top on the bases
steps.body: Fasten the finished top to your two bookcase bases with screws only — no glue — so the whole table comes apart for moving. Sand and finish the surface for craft use.

images.alt: Large craft table top with two drawers, resting on modular bookcase bases
